title: rosa canina subvar. oreades
intro: Rosa canina subvar. oreades, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and red hips. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subvar. oreades include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its ability to soothe and calm the skin and body. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and supporting the immune system, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia. Rosa canina subvar. oreades can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal creams and ointments to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
